_description: Geo risk map for SupplyLine. Each country gets a 1-10 risk score (10 = highest supply chain risk for photonics OEM programs) plus a photonics-specific concentration_note explaining why that risk exists in this industry.

_scoring_guide.1-3: Low risk. Politically stable, multi-supplier ecosystem, low concentration.
_scoring_guide.4-6: Moderate risk. Either single-country concentration, capacity constraints, or trade-policy friction.
_scoring_guide.7-8: High risk. Geopolitical exposure, single-fab dependencies, or active export controls.
_scoring_guide.9-10: Critical risk. Imminent disruption scenarios or near-monopoly concentration.

USA.concentration_note: Generally low political risk, but USA-based InP wafer fab capacity is a global bottleneck — only a handful of facilities (Coherent, Lumentum, IQE) serve the entire photonics industry. Single-facility outages cascade.

Taiwan.concentration_note: TSMC fabs ~60% of global leading-edge logic. Nearly every photonics ASIC and coherent DSP in this BOM (Broadcom BCM88690, Marvell Alaska, Acacia DSP, AMD/Xilinx FPGAs) traces back to TSMC. Taiwan-strait disruption is the binary scenario; day-to-day risk is allocation tightness during hyperscaler surges.

China.concentration_note: Major source of cost-competitive transceivers (InnoLight, Accelink, HiSense). US export controls, tariffs (Section 301), and end-customer flow-down restrictions (DoD, Tier-1 carriers) often disqualify Chinese-fab parts from defense and federal programs.

Japan.concentration_note: Stable, multi-supplier ecosystem. Murata and TDK dominate MLCC supply globally — a single Japan earthquake event historically tightens passive availability worldwide for ~2 quarters.

South Korea.concentration_note: Samsung and SK Hynix dominate DRAM/NAND supply. Geographic proximity to North Korea is a tail risk; trade friction with Japan periodically disrupts materials inputs.

Malaysia.concentration_note: Critical packaging/test hub for back-end semiconductor work; ON Semi, Infineon, Silicon Labs route many parts through here. Pandemic-era shutdowns (2021) demonstrated how a single Malaysian state can throttle global supply.

Philippines.concentration_note: Major packaging hub for Analog Devices, Nexperia, and several other catalog suppliers. Typhoon exposure and limited alternative back-end capacity create real disruption risk.

Thailand.concentration_note: Significant transceiver and HDD assembly base. Lumentum and several optical-component vendors finish product here. 2011 floods are the historical worst-case reference.

Germany.concentration_note: Infineon and Bosch base. Generally stable, but energy-cost shocks and automotive demand spikes can tighten power semi allocations on shared lines.

France.concentration_note: STMicroelectronics fab footprint. Stable, but ST allocation tightens reliably during automotive surges, which can squeeze general-purpose MCU availability in photonics BOMs.

Netherlands.concentration_note: ASML is the existential single-source for EUV lithography; while OEMs don't buy directly, every TSMC/Samsung roadmap depends on ASML deliveries. Also Nexperia (discrete) fab.
primary_risks: EUV bottleneck cascades down the stack

Ireland.concentration_note: Analog Devices and Intel both have wafer fabs here. Stable EU jurisdiction; small geographic footprint means single-site events are localized.

Morocco.concentration_note: ST packaging hub. Generally stable; tariff and customs friction can extend lead times.

Singapore.concentration_note: Major regional hub for distribution, wafer fab (GlobalFoundries Fab 7, Micron NAND), and back-end packaging. Politically stable, but a strategic chokepoint adjacent to South China Sea shipping lanes.

Finland.concentration_note: Nokia HQ and engineering base; not a fab country but a critical decision-making and integration node for Nokia photonics programs. NATO membership (2023) reduces prior border-risk concerns.

Canada.concentration_note: Ciena HQ region (Ottawa engineering) and several photonics startups; not a high-volume fab base. USMCA-protected trade with USA.

Sweden.concentration_note: Ericsson HQ; photonics design and systems engineering. Not a fab country. NATO membership (2024) reduces prior border-risk concerns.
